election_2026.title: 2026 General Elections
eligibility_rules: Must be a citizen of the country.
Must be at least 18 years of age on or before Election Day.
Must be a resident of the district for at least 30 days prior to the election.
Must not be currently serving a sentence for a felony conviction (laws vary by state).
identification_requirements: Government-issued photo ID (Driver's License, Passport, State ID).
If no photo ID: Utility bill, bank statement, or paycheck showing name and address (in some jurisdictions).

voting_methods.details: Vote at your assigned polling place on Nov 3, 2026, from 7:00 AM to 8:00 PM.
Participating locations open from Oct 19 to Oct 31.
Request by Oct 27. Must be postmarked by Election Day.

faq.question: Can I register on Election Day?
faq.answer: Only in participating states with Same-Day Registration (SDR). Check your local district rules.

faq.question: How do I find my polling place?
faq.answer: Use the VoxChain District Finder or visit the official Board of Elections website.

faq.question: What if I am out of the country?
faq.answer: You can vote using an Absentee Ballot under the UOCAVA act. Request your ballot at least 45 days before the election.

voxchain_technology.zk_proofs: VoxChain uses Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKP) to verify your eligibility without ever seeing your private data. A ZK proof allows you to prove you are over 18 and a resident without revealing your birthdate or address.
voxchain_technology.privacy_network: The underlying infrastructure is a data-protection blockchain that enables programmable privacy. It uses specialized smart contracts that can verify private data locally on your device.
voxchain_technology.privacy_guarantee: Your data never leaves your browser. The proof server runs locally (port 6300) and only the final 'verified' signal is sent to the blockchain.
